meta.title: Quenderin — An AI that runs on your phone, even offline
meta.description: Quenderin runs large language models fully on your device. No internet, no API keys, no telemetry. Download a model once and your assistant works on a plane, in a tent, off the grid.
lang.label: Language
hero.title: An AI that lives on your phone—not in someone’s cloud.
hero.lede: Quenderin runs large language models fully on your device. No internet, no API keys, no telemetry. Download a model once over Wi-Fi, and your assistant keeps working on a plane, in a tent, anywhere your signal doesn’t.
hero.dev.q: Best knot for a tarp shelter?
hero.dev.ai: A taut-line hitch on the ridgeline—it grips under load but slides to re-tension when the wind picks up. For the corners, a prusik loop lets you adjust without untying.
hero.dev.foot: 0 ms to a server · nothing left your phone
trust.label: Local inference, done right
problem.title: The cloud isn’t always there. Your AI shouldn’t vanish with your signal.
problem.body: Every other assistant is a thin client for a data center. Lose connection and it goes dark—and even when it works, your words travel to a server you don’t own. Quenderin flips that: the model lives on the device in your hand.
how.title: Download once. Run forever.
how.s1b: Quenderin reads your hardware—RAM, chip, GPU—and recommends the largest model your device can run well.
how.s2b: Pull the model once over Wi-Fi—0.4 to 4.7 GB, your choice. It resumes in the background if you get interrupted.
how.s3b: Everything runs locally from then on. A clear check tells you it’s safe before you leave the grid.
feat.title: A real assistant, with nothing phoning home.
feat.offline.b: After the one-time download, there are zero network calls. Works at 35,000 feet or three days into a hike.
feat.free.b: No subscriptions, no per-token billing. It runs on hardware you already own.
feat.agent.b: A tool-using agent with a hard safety blocklist—it will never autonomously touch:
feat.priv.b: No accounts, no analytics, no telemetry. Your conversations are yours.
feat.hw.b: It picks a model that fits—and warns you before one that doesn’t.
feat.os.b: MIT licensed. Read every line, audit every claim, build it yourself.
feat.resume.b: The model keeps downloading in the background—even if you switch apps the night before a trip.
story.eyebrow: Built for the edge of the map
story.title: You’re going off the grid for three days.
story.body: The night before, on hotel Wi-Fi, you download a model—and Quenderin keeps the download alive even when you switch apps, then shows a green “ready to go offline”. On the trail, with no bars, you still have a capable assistant in your pocket. That’s the whole point: an AI that doesn’t need the world to be online.
priv.title: Your data never had to leave. So it doesn’t.
priv.c1: No cloud calls after the model is downloaded
priv.c2: No API keys, no sign-in, no account
priv.c3: No analytics, no tracking, no telemetry
priv.c4: Conversations stored only on your device
priv.c5: Open source — verify all of the above yourself
priv.net.foot: 0 B sent since launch · nothing left your device
models.title: Bring any model. Right-sized for your hardware.
models.intro: Quenderin runs on llama.cpp—so it runs any GGUF model: Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Mistral, Gemma, Phi. Here’s the curated shortlist it recommends from, sized for everything from a Raspberry Pi to an M-series Mac.
models.r8: Best quality for a strong device
models.foot: + thousands more from Hugging Face—any GGUF works. Choosing is optional: Quenderin picks the best fit for your device automatically.
faq.title: Questions, answered.
faq.intro: It mostly comes down to one idea: the model runs on your device, so nothing leaves it. Here’s the rest.
faq.a1: Yes. Quenderin is MIT-licensed open source, and inference runs on your own hardware—so there are no token costs and no subscription.
faq.a2: No. After the one-time model download, Quenderin makes no network calls. There are no analytics, no accounts, and no telemetry. Because it’s open source, you can verify this yourself.
faq.q3: What devices does it run on?
faq.a3: The desktop app runs today on macOS and Linux. Native iOS and Android apps are in active development—the same hardware-detection and model-selection engine, rebuilt natively for performance.
faq.q4: Do I need a powerful phone?
faq.a4: No. Quenderin scales from a Raspberry Pi to an M-series Mac. It detects what your device can handle and recommends a model that actually runs—down to a 0.4 GB ultra-light build.
faq.a5: It’s open source and runnable from GitHub today. The polished mobile apps are on the way—star or watch the repo on GitHub to hear the moment they land.
cta.title: Bring your AI offline.
cta.body: It’s open source and runs from GitHub today. Star the repo to follow along—and watch releases to hear the moment the mobile apps land.
cta.consent: No email, no account, no trackers—just the code. See our Privacy Policy.
foot.note: No cookies, analytics, or trackers—nothing loads from a third party.
